initial_files.README.md:
# Acme storefront

The API accepts checkout requests. A separate worker consumes payment-provider webhooks. PostgreSQL stores orders and payment state.
initial_files.src/checkout/order-service.js: exports.createOrder = async request => ({ orderId: request.orderId, amount: request.amount });
initial_files.src/payments/provider-adapter.js: exports.authorize = async (orderId, amount) => provider.authorize({ merchantReference: orderId, amount });
initial_files.db/payment-schema.sql:
create table payment_attempts (order_id text, provider_status text);
create table payment_events (provider_event_id text primary key, handled_at timestamp);
initial_files.tests/payment-flow.test.js: // checkout authorization uses order_id as merchant reference; duplicate provider events are ignored

stages.prompt: Create the smallest useful initial SpecSpine for this brownfield storefront. Survey breadth before depth. Use specspine/payment-processing.md as the canonical, intentionally brief entry point for the payment responsibility so it can be deepened later. Capture both direct repository observations and any useful unconfirmed interpretation with an evidence baseline; do not manufacture decisions or constraints from code. Keep the map flat, qualitative, and incomplete. Do not modify repository files outside specspine/.

Deepen only the selected payment-processing area, starting from specspine/payment-processing.md. The relevant repository boundary is src/payments/**, src/checkout/payment-port.js, src/workers/payment-webhooks.js, config/payments.yml, db/payment-schema.sql, and tests/payment-flow.test.js. Inspect only representative evidence needed to explain authorization, webhook ingestion, persistence ownership, and the checkout boundary. Do not repeat the whole-repository survey or read reporting and unrelated checkout internals. Preserve observations versus unconfirmed interpretations, refresh the evidence baseline for rechecked observations, and update the smallest useful set under specspine/. Do not change source, tests, configuration, schema, or root documentation.
